source_text: The reverse fault RF-07 was identified in the Jurassic Fulmar Formation at a depth of 3500 m. Strike is 090° with a dip of 45° SE. High amplitude bright spot anomaly observed. Reflectors are continuous and sub-parallel. Velocity approximately 3800 m/s.

qc_recommendations: Add dip angle and direction for structural analysis.
Add strike azimuth for fault/structure orientation.
Include amplitude description for DHI (Direct Hydrocarbon Indicator) analysis.
source_text: A normal fault trending NE-SW was mapped at approximately 1800 ft depth. No formation data available. Dip estimated around 60°. Amplitude character is low and discontinuous.

qc_recommendations: Add dip angle and direction for structural analysis.
Add strike azimuth for fault/structure orientation.
Link feature to a named stratigraphic formation or horizon.
source_text: Strike-slip fault zone observed in the Cretaceous section. Chaotic reflector pattern with moderate amplitude. Feature extends from surface to 4.2 km depth.
